label: Trainer
description: A Pokémon Trainer is a person who catches, trains, cares for, and battles with Pokémon. Generally, Trainers possess multiple Pokémon, and interact with their world in unique, significant ways. In most settings, Trainers have their Pokémon with them at all times, with some manner to capture and store them, keeping them out of harm's way and keeping a small stable of Pokémon available at any given time, know as their Team.

label: Expedition Leader
description: Expedition Leaders, usually Aces, command an Exploration Team. Depending on the campaign setup, either every player is an Expedition Leader, with their own Teams, or the rest form the members of one player's Team. In a Mystery Dungeon setting, Expedition Leaders have fairly wide authority and varied specializations and goals from one another, pursing their own goals either with their own Team, or as part of (or possibly leading outright) an Explorer or Rescue Guild.

label: Ace
description: Ace creatures are generally PCs and PC grade creatures. Can be used for bosses, story significant entities, first partner creature etc. Awards more skill points at character creation and possibly other effects.

Unlike other creatures, Rayquaza does not require a partner with a Key Stone to use Mega Evolve, only requiring Dragon Ascent to be on its Active List.

Creatures with [Aura Sense] automatically succeed on Spot Hidden checks to detect illusions.

description: This creature is able to reduce their size. By spending 2 PP, the user can add or remove the Shrink affliction from itself.

label: Silent
description: This creature is capable of moving without making a sound. Listen checks to detect this creature are Almost Impossible.

label: Slippery
description: This creature is hard to pin down, and makes Grapple checks against them Intense. If this creature is affected by Grapple, they have a +30 bonus to escape the Grapple.

label: Slumbering Statue
description:
Zacian/Zamazenta unique trait. Able to turn into statues/gargoyles to hibernate/slumber.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Speech
description: This creature is capable of coherent speech. Can be awarded to PCs with the [Creature] trait in games that include Human characters.

label: Spirit Traveler
description:
This creature is capable of turning into a spirit and temporarily leaving its body.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Split Evolution
description: Indicates this creature can evolve into more than one option.

label: Shed Evolution
description:
When Nincada evolves into Ninjask, if another Capture Container is available, a Shedinja will take its residence inside of said container.
The GM should spawn in a new Shedinja and allow the player to freely capture it.

label: Steel Officer
description:
Leadership checks made to influence this creature suffer -20 for creatures with the [Steel Pawn] trait, and +30 for creatures with the [Steel Shogun] Trait.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Steel Pawn
description:
Leadership checks made to influence this creature gain +20 for creatures with the [Steel Officer] trait, and +50 for creatures with the [Steel Shogun] Trait.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Steel Shogun
description:
This creature cannot be targeted by Leadership checks from creatures with the [Steel Pawn] and [Steel Officer] traits.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Stormcaller
description: This creature can create or clear stormy conditions. The user has access to the [Stormcaller] Tutor List.

label: Mouthy
description: This creature has an advanced and developed mouth (or something resembling one) that allows them to bite, chew, rip and tear with decent offensive effectiveness. The user has access to the [Jaw] Tutor List.

label: Tailed
description: This creature has a sizeable tail. The user has access to the [Tailed] Tutor List.

The creature may originate the Use, Draw/Store, Drop, Bestow, Grab, Fling, and Struggle from a space within Xm of the creature. description:
This creature is able to communicate telepathically. The creature gains the Attack Mind Reader as a Learned Attack, and can use the Mind Reading Action.

label: Teleport
description: Pertaining to Moves and actions that involve Teleportation.

label: Teleporter
description: This creature gains Teleporting movement equal to their highest movement score. The creature gains the Teleport attack as a Learned Attack.

label: Therian
description: This creature is in a unbound, bestial Forme. This removes the restraints on the user's powers, and engaging in combat may drive the creature into a furious rampage. The use of a Reveal Glass transforms this creature into its [Therian] Forme.

This creature is capable of controlling time.

description:
This creature can time-travel. This is a powerful narrative ability and needs to be well regulated.

This creature is capable of removing pollution and poisons out of the natural environment and creatures living there over the course of a Span whilst camping.

description:
This creature has been flung through Ultra Space, and appear to resonate in one form or another with Z-Energy. These creatures tend to be quite formidable. These creatures also possess [Ultra].
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Ultra Burst
description:
This creature is in a unique Forme after ingesting colossal amounts of energy. The creature consumes a large amount of energy to maintain this Forme, consuming 8 Ticks of PP per Daytime Span spent in the Forme, and 1 Tick of PP per Danger Span accumulated.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Ultra Wormhole
description:
This creature is capable of breaking the barrier between the current universe/dimension and Ultra Space, opening and closing Ultra Wormholes in exchange for 10 PP. They may traverse the interdimensional void with the Movement Allowance of their choice, protecting creatures within a 3m radius from the void's hazards and propelling them alongside the user through the void.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Ultra
description: A trait used to identify creatures whom traversed Ultra Space, or originate from the interdimensional void itself.

label: Underdog
description: An creature with lower base stats, making them eligible for Underdog perks. The 'Underdog Cap' is a total of 510 BST.

label: Unown Choir
description:
The Unown are able to do a lot of weird shit when they sing together.

label: Volatile Bomb

This creature is capable of locating clean water.

This creature can naturally purify water on contact.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Waterwalker
description:
This creature may choose to walk on water instead of swimming in it. They may stand on the surface of water and more across it using their Overland Movement Allowance, rather than Swim.

label: Weapon Training
description: This creature is trained in the use of human-made weaponry. NOTE: NEEDS AUTOMATION TO OFFSET WEAPON PENALTY.

label: Weapon
description: This trait applies to weapons and weapon-interactive moves. NOTE: NEEDS AUTOMATION FOR INNATE PENALTY.

label: Blade
description: This weapons possesses a sharp, edged surface that the user is able to utilize for the weapons' Attacks, which usually have @Trait[sharp].

label: Blunt
description: This weapon is weighty and solid, making it ideal for brute force physical Attacks, often being @Trait[hammer] Attacks.

label: Projectile
description: This weapon, via a chemical, mechanical, or physical method, launches physical projectiles as its intended form of Attack, usually possessing @Trait[missile].

label: Bomb
description: This weapon utilizes a powerful reaction to produce explosions, utlizing the shockwaves and fragmentation as its primary vector of Attack. These weapons' Attacks often possess @Trait[explode].

label: DEW
description: This weapon is a directed energy weapon, or 'DEW', which compresses fundamental energies in a tight, controlled beam that is used to inflict @Trait[ray] damage.

label: Emission
description: This weapon expels energy or matter toward targets, usually less controlled as a DEW, but often in more signficiant quantities. This weapon's attacks are likely to possess @Trait[pulse].

label: Firearm
description: This trait refers to guns and other such @Trait[projectile] devices that utilize an explosive charge to propel one or more projectiles down a barrel to inflict damage on a target. Firearms tend to be synonymous with more cold and calculated modern-style warfare, away from the romanticized sword and spear combat of the Middle Ages and Antiquity, and are ubiquitious in many settings that resemble contemporary society to any degree.

label: Weathervane
description:
The creature changes Forme depending on the most recent Weather, changing %lt;Type>and granting the appropriate %lt;Adaptable Type> Trait.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Wielder
description: This creature receives a Wielded/Held Item slot for free.

label: Willpower Eraser
description:
This creature is capable of removing others' willpower and motivation.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Winged
description:
This creature gains a flying speed by way of wings, requiring exertion to continue flight. Grants access to the [Winged] Tutor List.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Wool Production
description: TO BE DETERMINED

label: X Ray Vision
description:
This creature can see through walls.
SUBJECT TO CHANGE

label: Z Crystal Creation
description: The creature's body can shed prismatic shards that are capable of drawing, storing, and unleashing massive quanities of energy. As a Crafting Project, the user (Challenging, Occult: Legendary) or the creature that's responsible for them (Hard Husbandry) can craft a Z-Crystal or Z-Power-Crystal, matching the %lt;Type>of a creature nearby it or with the %lt;Type>and <Species> of a creature nearby it in the case of a Z-Power-Crystal. Otherwise, a variant of either variety of Crystal is rolled and generated randomly.

label: Tera Power
description: This creature emanates Terastal Energy, able to direct it toward other creatures or siphon it away from its surroundings into itself. This creature, through great strain, can induce Terastallization in @Trait[pokemon].

label: Fan Dex
description: This item originates from a fan-developed project, usually a romhack or fangame, and was adapted by the 2e Dev Team for use in this system.

label: Dev Dex
description: This item originates from a member of the 2e Dev Team, and is approved for use in the system. These items are generally adapted from an existing item or concept (officially or fanmade), and refined for default inclusion in this system.

label: Digimon
description: Not quite a @Trait[pokemon], creatures with this trait are 'digital monsters' or 'Digimon' for short. Creatures with this trait usually have the Digitize Trait, and always have access to the Digimon Perk Web. Other items with this trait are specifically designed for use by creatures with this trait, and games that center around Digimon and its settings.

label: Homebrew
description: This item is not part of the core 2e module. GMs that distribute homemade Species, Gear, Attacks, Abilities, Perks, etc. should include this trait on those items. GMs and Players in possession of these items should be wary using them, as they may not be designed to work perfectly with the system.

label: Stellar
description: This creature may assume a heightened Forme when exposed to a Tera Orb, causing it to bristle with Stellar power. While in Stellar Forme, the user's control of Terastal Energy expands and is refined. The user may manipulate Terastal Energy to deactivate other creatures' Terastallization and even create physical constructs with it, such as walls and barriers.

label: Fling
description: Denotes if an item can be used as part of the Fling Attack Action, or if an Action is a Fling Action. Flung items or creatures that would miss their target Scatter, being relocated to a space a random distance away from the target creature/space in a random direction. The Scatter Direction is determined with a d8, interating clockwise from 1 (to the map's 'north' of the target) to 8 (to the map's 'northwest' of the target). The Scatter Distance is dX meters in the rolled Direction away from the target, being based on which Range Increment the target was in, times 2 - RI1 is d2, RI2 is d4, RI3 is d6, and RI4 is d8.

label: Combat
description: Denotes the general use of a item or piece of gear. [Consumable] items cannot be used in combat if they lack this trait.

label: Consumable
description: Items with this trait are destroyed when they are used. Items with @Trait[stack-x] remove one item from their Stack, until the count hits zero, and then the item is destroyed.

label: Battle
description: Items with this trait are particularly designed for combat use. These items may used be Used during Combat.

label: Ball
description: Items with this trait are spherical and perfectly sized to be thrown. Gear with self-compression and miniaturization technology, such as Poke Balls and Pester Balls, usually possess this trait.

label: Concoction
description: Denotes if an item was created or improved with the wiles of a tricksy madlad. Generally, these tend to be much more suited for combat, and characteristically may backfire spectacularly.

label: Berry
description: These items are small to medium sized (usually smaller than an adult human's clenched fist) pulpy fruits that lack stones or pits. Generally, these @Trait[food] items are beloved by @Trait[pokemon] and many other creatures besides, and have significant nutritional and combat benefits.

label: Natural
description: Denotes if an item is biological in nature, and is naturally-occurring.

label: Food
description: Denotes if an item is edible to most creatures. When @Trait[consumable] items with this Trait are Flung, they are Used on the target of the Fling.

label: Restorative
description: Denotes if an item is medicinal in use or application. While a number of @Trait[natural] items have this trait, a number of manufactured items do as well.

label: Capture
description: Gear with this trait are able to Capture creatures, usually adding them to the user's Party as a result. However, the specifics of how this gear is able to do so and what happens with and resulting from that Capturing can differ between different sorts of gear.

label: X Strike
description:
Denotes if a damaging attack hits multiple times.
These attacks still roll a single Accuracy and Damage roll against one target as per normal, but deals damage as if it hit 1+d(X-1) times.
On-hit effects still consider this as a single attack.

If a shield breaks before the final damage hit is applied, the remaining damage hits will apply as normal.

label: Double-Strike
description:
Whenever you declare an Attack with this trait, you may choose to resolve it twice without expending its Action Cost, changing targets if desired.
Paying PP & resolving effects for each of the two attacks as normal.

label: Adaptable
description: Means that an attack or action can be Type-Shifted based on other Traits.

label: Arcane
description: Denotes magical actions.

label: Basic
description: Means than an attack or action is simple/reliable, generally associated with a low PP Cost.

label: Blast X
description: Utilized with ranged attacks and effects. Affects entities within a X+1m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 1
description: Affects entities within a 2m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 2
description: Affects entities within a 3m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 3
description: Affects entities within a 4m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 4
description: Affects entities within a 5m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 5
description: Affects entities within a 6m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 6
description: Affects entities within a 7m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 7
description: Affects entities within a 8m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 8
description: Affects entities within a 9m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 9
description: Affects entities within a 10m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 10
description: Affects entities within a 11m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 11
description: Affects entities within a 12m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Blast 12
description: Affects entities within a 1m radius circle/sphere, centered on a selected space.

label: Bone
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a bone or ossified item or appendage.

label: Contact
description: Denotes if a creature makes contact with its target through using the associated Action or Attack. The Range of the Action or Attack is affected by the user's Reach - set as REACH+RANGE-1.

label: Crash 1/2
description: On miss, the user takes damage equal to half the amount the attack would have dealt to the target.

label: Crit 1
description: User's CRIT stage is considered to be +1 higher when using the associated action or attack.

label: Crit 2
description: User's CRIT stage is considered to be +2 higher when using the associated action or attack.

label: Crit 3
description: User's CRIT stage is considered to be +3 higher when using the associated action or attack.

label: Crit 4
description: User's CRIT stage is considered to be +4 higher when using the associated action or attack.

label: Crushing
description: The associated action/attack deals double damage to and have +20 Accuracy against creatures that are Shrinked, or creatures that are 2 Size Categories or more smaller than the user.

label: Curl
description: Denotes actions/attacks that require the user to curl their body into a ball or another round shape.

label: Dance
description: Denotes actions/attacks that utilize dancing.

label: Danger Close
description: These attacks bypass the Accuracy Check within the first Range Increment, and start at 100 Accuracy from the second Band onward.

label: Dash
description:
Actions with this Trait may be declared even if the user is out of range of a target, such as those with @Trait[contact] and the target is out of the user's Reach. When declaring this Action, the user freely uses Rush, Leap, Jump, or Breach, and utilizes the gained Movement.
If after this extra Movement the user is still out of range or the Action otherwise would be unable to be used, the Action fails and any Activation, PP, or other costs are still consumed as normal. If the user is in range, the Action continues its resolution as normal.

label: Defensive
description: Denotes a defensive attack/ability.

label: Defrost
description:
The user may declare Actions with this Trait while they have the @Affliction[frozen] Affliction, curing themselves of the Affliction on declaration.

Whenever you use an Action with this Trait while suffering from @Affliction[frostbite], you may cure up to 5 stacks of Frostbite on yourself.

Targets hit by Actions with this Trait are cured of @Affliction[frozen] and up to 5 stacks of @Affliction[frostbite] after the action resolves, any damage dealt ignores the 33% damage reduction from the @Affliction[frozen] Affliction.

label: Drain X
description: On hit, the user heals X of the amount the attack deals to a creature. X is usually a fraction, ranging from 1/16 at minimum and 3/4 at maximum.

label: Drain 1/16
description: On hit, the user heals 1/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 1/8
description: On hit, the user heals 1/8 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 3/16
description: On hit, the user heals 3/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 1/4
description: On hit, the user heals 1/4 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 5/16
description: On hit, the user heals 5/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 3/8
description: On hit, the user heals 3/8 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 7/16
description: On hit, the user heals 7/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 1/2
description: On hit, the user heals 1/2 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 9/16
description: On hit, the user heals 9/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 5/8
description: On hit, the user heals 5/8 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 11/16
description: On hit, the user heals 11/16 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Drain 3/4
description: On hit, the user heals 3/4 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Draw In
description: The attack/ability draws in other attacks and abilities within a specific range or meeting specific criteria

label: Earthbound
description: The attack/ability hits all creatures in area-of-effect, so long as the area-of-effect affects surfaces/objects are connected or touching, so a creature sitting on a tree branch is hit if a creature on the ground below is targeted and the tree itself is in the affected area. Attacks with this trait are one step more effective against creatures with the Grounded Affliction.

label: Environ
description: Denotes if an attack/ability is affected by or interacts with [Weather] or [Terrain] effects.

label: Execute
description: On hit, an affected creature has their HP reduced to zero.

label: Explode
description: Denotes if an attack/ability consists of an explosion of some kind.

label: Field
description: Denotes if an attack has a particular use out of combat.

label: Friendly
description: These actions and attacks do not affect allied creatures in range.

label: Flinch
description: A marker for moves that can afflict Flinch on others

label: Flinch XY
description: The creature that uses this action is Flinched, having their next Activation delayed by XY%.

label: Hammer
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a sturdy, weighted object or limb swung at high speeds.

label: Hazard
description:
Denotes if an attack/ability/effect creates or interacts with Hazards. Hazards are usually static objects occupying a single space on the ground or another surface that cause detrimental effects when a creature passes through that space. Hazards last the duration of a combat unless destroyed prematurely or otherwise specified in their source's effect.
One such effect that can remove Hazards prematurely is the @UUID[Compendium.ptr2e.core-moves.Item.ypqvBMddHC2zIDib]{Sweep Action}

label: Healing
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect interacts with healing the user or others.

label: Horn
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a horn or a similar prominent pointy appendage.

label: Interrupt X
description: Denotes that an Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "X", provided the conditions for using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 1
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "1", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 2
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "2", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 3
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "3", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 4
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "4", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 5
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "5", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 6
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "6", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Interrupt 7
description: This Action may be used as an Interrupt at hierachy tier "7", provided the conditions to using the Interrupt are met.

label: Jaw
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes the user's mouth/teeth.

label: Kick
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes at least one of the user's feet.

label: Light
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes or produces light.

label: Linked
description: Denotes if an attack is linked to the use of another. Linked attacks are automatically added to the Active List, ignoring the Active List limit.

label: Magma Wader
description: Can move through Magma tiles without suffering the negative effects

label: Manipulate
description: Denotes if an action or attack manipulates another creature or object.

label: Mascot
description: Denotes powerful or useful attacks/abilities, generally only obtainable by a creature that has obtained a strong bond with comrades, a mentor, and/or a Trainer.

label: Max
description: These attacks deal 1/4 of the damage they would deal if they are negated by a @Trait[shield] effect and apply their secondary effects. These attacks also possess [Danger Close] and [Smite]. These attacks may only be used while Dynamaxed/Gigantimaxed/Eternamaxed.

label: Melee
description: Denotes attacks utilized by Melee Weapons. These attacks also possess @Trait[contact].

label: Missile
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a discrete, high-speed projectile.

label: Move Locked
description: Denotes if an attack is species-exclusive. Move-Locked attacks are automatically added to the Active List, ignoring the Active List limit.

label: Pass X
description: This attack allows the creature to move through enemy squares as part of their Movement and attack those creatures. They can attack up to X creatures beyond the first target, declaring against all targets and resolving the attack against them simultaneously as though the move were an AOE move. When doing so, the user must Move and must 'pass' through these targets' spaces (treating those spaces as empty during this Movement) and end this 'pass' on an opposite side of the target in space(s) they can occupy, and cannot have Attack of Opportunity declared in response to this Movement and any related Movement made while Moving between Pass targets.

label: Pledge
description: Denotes a special Pledge attack, which increases in intensity and may produce a special combination when used in tandem with another Pledge attack.

label: Powder
description: Denotes if an attack or ability spreads or uses powder or a fine dust.

label: priority
description: A marker for moves that can afflict Advance on others

label: Priority XY
description: The creature that uses this action is Advanced, having their next Activation advanced by XY%.

label: Pulse
description: Denotes if an attack or ability expels a wave of energy.

label: Punch
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes at least one of the user's hands.

label: Ranged
description: Denotes attacks utilized by Ranged Weapons.

label: Ray

label: Recoil-X
description: On hit, the user takes damage equal to X of the amount the attack deals to a creature. X is usually a fraction.

label: Recoil 1/8
description: On hit, the user takes damage equal to 1/8 of the amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Recoil 1/4

label: Recoil 1/3

label: Recoil 1
description: On hit, the user takes damage equal to the full amount the attack deals to a creature.

label: Room
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect interacts with Rooms. Rooms apply their effects to all creatures on the Field.

label: Set-Up
description: The action has a two-step activation and resolution, split across two activations (Set-Up and Resolution) and consumes Complex Actions on both Activations, but PP only once. It may have separate effects on both the set up activation and resolution activation, though usually the attack's accuracy and damage roll occur on the resolution activation. Set-up moves temporarily gain @Trait[grapple] during the resolution phase.

label: Sharp
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a sharp, edged limb or weapon.

label: Shield
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect provides a shielding function. The HP-like mechanic of Shields is considered to possess this Trait.

label: Shrink
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect provides an effect that reduces a creature's Size Category.

label: Sky
description: The attack's area of effect extends vertically, up to the nearest ceiling and down to the nearest surface (max 20m each direction)

label: Social
description: Denotes if an attack or ability features social interaction.

label: Sonic
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes sound.

label: Substitute
description: Denotes if an attack or ability creates a Substitute or similar mitigating method.

label: Summon
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect features or interacts with a Summon.

label: Tail
description: Denotes if an attack or ability utilizes a tail or a similar lengthy appendage.

label: Terrain
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect interacts with Terrains. Terrains apply their effects to all creatures on the Field that are in contact with a surface, while creatures in the air or in a liquid (whether floating/ traversing naturally or by some other effect) are not affected.

label: Transform
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect Transforms the user.

label: Trap
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect traps creatures, usually via the Bound Affliction.

label: Unreliable
description: This attack's Accuracy is equal to 30+User Level-Target Level, and is unmodified by and ignores all other changes to actions' Accuracy or the target's Evasion, including from [Danger-Close].

label: Weather
description: Denotes if an attack/ability/effect interacts with Weathers.

label: Wind
description: Denotes if an attack or ability interacts with or utilizes Wind.

label: Zenith
description: These attacks deal 1/4 of the damage they would deal if they are negated by a @Trait[shield] effect.

label: Connection
description: Denotes if an Ability or Perk provides an Attack onto a creature's Active List, not taking up a slot on the user's Active List.

label: Fusion
description: Denotes if an Ability governs a fusion between 2 or more creatures.

label: Innate
description: This Ability cannot be copied, removed, or nullified.

label: Major Affliction
description: Major Afflictions persist during a fight; even if the creature is Recalled.

label: Minor Affliction
description: Minor Afflictions get automatically removed when a creature is Recalled.

label: Pseudo Affliction
description: Pseudo Afflictions are effects that are similar in nature to Major/Minor afflictions, but count as neither; and often have unique interactions with other effects.

label: Stage change
description: Denotes if an effect alters a creature's Stat stages.

label: Desperation X
description: An effect activates when the user's health is below X of their Max HP. The effect is usually listed in the source's effect descripion. For Gear, such as Berries, this means they can only be used when meeting the trigger conditions for [Desperation X].

label: Desperation 1/2
description: An effect activates when the user's health is below 1/2 of their Max HP. The effect is usually listed in the source's effect descripion. For Gear, such as Berries, this means they can only be used when meeting the trigger conditions for [Desperation 1/2].

label: Desperation 1/3
description: An effect activates when the user's health is below 1/3 of their Max HP. The effect is usually listed in the source's effect descripion. For Gear, such as Berries, this means they can only be used when meeting the trigger conditions for [Desperation 1/3].

label: Desperation 1/4

label: Desperation 3/4
description: An effect activates when the user's health is below 3/4 of their Max HP. The effect is usually listed in the source's effect descripion. For Gear, such as Berries, this means they can only be used when meeting the trigger conditions for [Desperation 3/4].

label: Intrepid X
description: An effect activates when the user's health is above X of their Max HP. The effect is usually listed in the source's effect descripion. for Gear, such as Armor, this means they can only be used when meeting the trigger conditions for [Intrepid X].

label: Reload X
description: When this item's Charge Pool is depleted, a empty Reload Clock specific to this item, X Spans in size, is created. Creatures can use the Reload Default Action on the item to tick this Clock up. When the Clock is filled, the item's Charge Pool is refilled. Reloading a weapon either consumes an Ammunition item relating to the weapon, or consumes IP based on the weapon's rarity and other Traits.

description: When this item's Charge Pool is depleted, a empty Reload Clock specific to this item, 19 Spans in size, is created. Creatures can use the Reload Default Action on the item to tick this Clock up. When the Clock is filled, the item's Charge Pool is refilled. Reloading a weapon either consumes an Ammunition item relating to the weapon, or consumes IP based on the weapon's rarity and other Traits.

label: Reload 20
description: When this item's Charge Pool is depleted, a empty Reload Clock specific to this item, 20 Spans in size, is created. Creatures can use the Reload Default Action on the item to tick this Clock up. When the Clock is filled, the item's Charge Pool is refilled. Reloading a weapon either consumes an Ammunition item relating to the weapon, or consumes IP based on the weapon's rarity and other Traits.

label: Readied
description: Actions with his trait cannot be used if the user has Move since the end of their Previous Activation, and cannot Move during the Activation this Action is used. `Moving` includeds the user's own Movement, Forced Movement, or as Rider or Crew to a creature or vehicle that would Move.

label: Manned
description: This trait denotes if a weapon is mostly stationary, being affixed to an emplacement, structure, or vehicle. Creatures must use the weapon from its designated postiion, and cannot easily Move the weapon by themselves.

label: Autoload
description: Weapons with this trait use a mechanism to automatically feed their ammunition, rather than loading each shot by hand, like with a sling, bow, or muzzleloading firearm.

label: Ranger
description: Creature trait for denoting 'Ranger' type characters.

label: Explorer
description: Creature trait for denoting 'Explorers' in a PMD-style Campaign.

label: Pokemon
description: This is a Pokemon.

label: Humanoid
description: This is a Humanoid.

label: Masked Oni
description: The creature has a special bond with a number of masks, ones that are specifically crafted with Tera Crystals. The creature may draw power from these masks in order to Terastallize.

label: Grapple
description: Denotes if an Action can be utilized while the Action's user is affected by the Grapple affliction. All [Grapple] actions are considered to have the @Trait[contact] Trait whenever they are used as part of a Grapple.

label: Pierce
description: Denotes if an Action clears [Shield] effects on affected target(s). This happens when the Action hits the target(s) and before damage and other on-hit effects from the Action occur.

label: Phenomenon
description: Denotes if an Item, Ability, or Effect relates to the various transformational phenomena, ie Mega Evolution.

label: Conventional
description: Gear with this Trait generally appear in the generic, mostly "@Trait[modern] with hints of @Trait[sci-fi]" setting in which most mainline Pokémon games and anime are set. Often, these are also @Trait[league] games.

label: Mystery Dungeon
description: Gear with this Trait are intended for use in Mystery Dungeon campaigns, and are not recommended for use in non-Mystery Dungeon games.

label: Modern
description: Gear with this Trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from the modern era, typifying products and inventions from the late 1800s to the modern day, covering the turn of the century, the World Wars, the Cold War, and our present day.

label: Early Modern
description: Gear with this Trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from the early modern period, typifying products and inventions from the early 1500s to the late 1800s, covering the Ages of Exploration and Imperialism, and the dawn of the industrial era.

label: Medieval
description: Gear with this Trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from the postclassical period, typifying products and inventions from the early 500s to the early 1500s, covering the Middle Ages.

label: Ancient
description: Gear with this Trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from the ancient period, typifying products and inventions from around 3000 BCE to the early 500s CE, covering the dawn of civilization through the end of Late Antiquity.

label: Sci-Fi
description: Gear with this trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from alternate present or speculative future times, typifying products and inventions that boast significantly advanced technology and potentially aliens or other dimensions.

label: Fantasy
description: Gear with this trait are generally from, readily found in, or other roughly analogous to Gear from storybooks, myths, and legends across any sort of time, typifying products and inventions that are attuned to the arcane or occult beyond what is seen in our reality.

label: Style
description: This Action is a Style. Style Actions apply a @Trait[pseudo-affliction] on the user, or the user may spend +2 PP to apply the effect to an allied creature in range. When a [Style] with a different name is applied to a creature that already possesses a [Style] effect, the existing effect is replaced with the new effect.

label: Modification
description: Whenever you declare an Attack, you can modify the Attack with this effect, by paying the appropriate PP cost listed in the [Modification]'s Effect, after which you apply the [Modification]'s Effect to this Attack before it continues to resolve. You cannot declare multiple Modification effects in response to a single declared Attack.

label: Command
description: This Trait denotes Actions that are of a commanding nature (naturally), generally associated with instruction, tactics, and the like. At default, Command Actions are only able to target their user or creature in their Team. Notably, Command Actions are one of a small selection of Actions allowed during League Combat.

label: Evolution
description: TO BE DETERMINED

label: Closed-Heart
description:
Creatures with this Trait possess a heart that's been forcefully closed, gaining the following changes:
1) +50% SPD
2) The Extra Abilities "Hyper Agressive" and "Coldhearted"
3) +1 Active List Slot
4) Access to the [Shadow] %lt;Type>Learnlist
5) The Anguish Skill (100 by default) and its associated mechanics
6) Are susceptible to the "Hyper Mode" Affliction
7) Cannot undergo Evolution or other miscellaneous transformations
8. Placed onto a slower EXP Track, based on their Species

label: Ignore Immunity
description:
Denotes if an Effect should ignore an actor's Immunity. This trait ignores all Actor based immunities while applying an effect.

If you want to be more specific, you can add a custom [Ignore Immunity X] trait, such as [Ignore Immunity Poison] to denote that the effect ignores Poison Immunity.

label: Ignore Type Immunity
description: Denotes if an Attack should ignore effectiveness Immunity and instead set type based effectiveness to 0.

label: Use Opponent's Stats
description: An attack with this Trait will calculate the Offensive Stat using the Opponent's stats instead of the user's.

label: Requires Rework
description: The current version of this requires a rework, and will be changed or removed in the near future

label: Uncapturable
description: This creature cannot be captured by any means.

label: Minor Buff
description: [Minor Buff] perks, also known as 'Spacer Perks' are perks which are not present on the web, and instead are widely available through one of the many @UUID[Compendium.ptr2e.core-perks.Item.minorbuffperk000] perks present on the web. You can use the Compendium Browser to find a full list of options, or check the Minor Buff folder in the PTR 2e Perks Compendium.

label: DoT
description: This Affliction is one that Deals Damage over Time (DoT).

label: Control
description: This Affliction is one which includes Crowd Control effects.

label: Flat
description: This Trait denotes that the Attack deals Flat Damage.

label: Vaccine
description: Gain +50% Damage versus Virus-trait Digimon. Inflict 50% less Damage vs Data-trait Digimon.

label: Data
description: Gain +50% Damage versus Vaccine-trait Digimon. Inflict 50% less Damage vs Virus-trait Digimon.

label: Virus
description: Gain +50% Damage versus Data-trait Digimon. Inflict 50% less Damage vs Vaccine-trait Digimon.

label: Free
description: Neutrally aligned Digimon do not deal or receive more or less damage from any other Digimon Traits.

label: In Training
description: In Training Digimon are one step up from Baby Digimon, but are still weak and undefined. Starter Digimon are sometimes given at this stage.

label: Rookie
description: Rookie Digimon are capable of fighting at this stage, and usually gain traits defining them as Vaccine, Virus, or Data.

label: Champion
description: Champion Digimon are grown up and powerful, and are often the strongest Digimon you meet in the wild.

label: Ultimate
description: Ultimate Digimon have gone beyond the typical level of power, often becoming leaders of communities or petty tyrants of swathes of land.

label: Mega
description: Mega Digimon have powers that if misused could destroy their world. In some cases they are the servants of God.  Or are worshipped as gods themselves.

label: Ultra (Digimon)
description: Ultra Digimon are true rarities, being temporary states of Mega Digimon, or DNA Fusions of multiple Mega Digimon.

label: UFO
description: This Trait denotes the creature to be or be in a Unidentified Flying Object (UFO). Meaning they must use Pilot (Aerospace) for its Rush skill.

label: Agitator
description: This creature or item is specialised in the art of Agitation, using social moves to debuff their opponents.

label: Fate
description: Actions, Effects or Mechanics with this trait alter the result of a Roll. You can only ever apply 1 @Trait[fate] effect on any given roll. This includes mechanics like rerolling using 'Push Your Luck' or spending Luck on a roll using 'Fortune'.
